education.notes: Per SEC filing: attended Harvard 1956-1957; left without completing degree. His father John Rodman Hooker (HD3243) was Harvard A.B. 1925.
Per SEC filing: 1966-1967, graduate work without completing degree.

Per SEC filing: M.F.A. 1995. The art-historical pivot late in his career may echo the Hooker family's artistic + scientific pattern (great-aunt Marian Osgood Hooker H04280 the photographer-physician; mother Josephine Dunham's Chicago social arts circle).

careers.notes: Per SEC filing 2013. Office: 750 Battery Street, 6th Floor, SF CA 94111.
Per SEC filing 2013: "Senior Advisor 2009 – Present". Sam confirms ongoing role.

narrative_blocks.header: Carries his Shreve grandfather's surname as middle
narrative_blocks.body: Tony was born in 1938 to John Rodman Hooker and his second wife Josephine Dunham. His middle name 'Shreve' honors his maternal grandfather George Choate Shreve, founder of Shreve & Co., considered San Francisco's oldest commercial establishment.

narrative_blocks.header: USNA 1961 then SF Art Institute MFA
narrative_blocks.body: He graduated the US Naval Academy with a B.S. in 1961 after a year at Harvard. Three decades later he earned an M.F.A. from the San Francisco Art Institute in 1995, the arts-and-finance dual track distinguishing him among Bay Area money managers.

narrative_blocks.header: Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners since 1986
narrative_blocks.body: Since 1986 Tony has been Principal and Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners in San Francisco, holding the Senior Advisor role since 2009. His decades there overlapped with the 2014 arrival of his godson Lyman Domning Howard.

narrative_blocks.header: Godfather to distant cousin Lyman Howard
narrative_blocks.body: He has served as godfather to Lyman Domning Howard (b. 1969), a distant Hooker cousin descended through Bessie Augusta Hooker Lent. Lyman joined Tony's firm Ashfield Capital Partners as Portfolio Manager in 2014.

narrative_blocks.header: Steward of three generations of Hooker pedigree
narrative_blocks.body: He has assembled and preserves much of the modern Hooker pedigree, drawing on family papers and his own research. The pedigree work has been a primary source for genealogical research connecting separate cousin branches.

cross_connections.display_label: Godfather to distant cousin who joined his Ashfield firm.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Shreve is his paternal grandmother's maiden surname, preserved in his name per the family naming pattern. His father John Rodman Hooker (HD3243) was Elizabeth's son.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Shreve is his paternal grandmother's maiden surname, preserved in his name per the family naming pattern. His father John Rodman Hooker (HD3243) was Elizabeth's son.

bio.extra_names: Named after his great-uncle John Daggett Hooker (H02709), the Hooker Telescope donor

marriages.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: married Josephine Dunham in 1934. ONE SON: Anthony Shreve Hooker (b.1938, living, NOT built per database living-person rule). Anthony — known as 'Tony' — is Sam Talcott Hooker's first cousin once removed (colloquially 'uncle'). Tony graduated US Naval Academy B.S. 1961 + San Francisco Art Institute M.F.A. 1995; Principal/Portfolio Manager at Ashfield & Co./Ashfield Capital Partners 1986-present. Tony has been a major source for the Hooker family pedigree.

narrative_blocks.header: Named after his telescope-donor great-uncle
narrative_blocks.body: He was named for his father's uncle John Daggett Hooker, who had built the iron-and-oil fortune that funded the 100-inch Hooker Telescope at Mount Wilson. Tony Hooker preserves a photo with his father's inscription: 'uncle of my father, after whom I was named.'

narrative_blocks.header: Harvard A.B. 1925; Hooker & Fay senior partner
narrative_blocks.body: After Harvard A.B. 1925 he became senior partner of Hooker & Fay brokerage at 340 Pine Street San Francisco in 1929 — just before the October crash. He chaired the SF Stock Exchange Finance Committee in 1948-1949.

narrative_blocks.header: Sailed transatlantic with wife Josephine multiple times
narrative_blocks.body: He and his second wife Josephine Dunham made multiple transatlantic voyages between 1934 and 1955, sailing the SS Santa Rosa, the Queen Mary, and others. He lived through Hillsborough, San Mateo, and Sonoma before his 1983 death in Riverside.

narrative_blocks.header: Father of Anthony 'Tony' Shreve Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: His only son Anthony Shreve Hooker, born 1938, became Principal and Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners and the primary contemporary documentor of the Hooker pedigree. Tony's middle name carries forward his maternal grandfather Shreve's family — founders of San Francisco's oldest commercial establishment.

cross_connections.display_label: Named in honor of his telescope-funding great-uncle.

education.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: graduated 1921. Member of Alumni Association; later President of the Board of Trustees of his alma mater. The Santa Barbara School was a college-preparatory boys' school of the era.

Per Golden Nugget Library: A.B. 1925. The 1921 RMS Aquitania voyage to Europe (London → Italy → Switzerland) was Harvard summer or pre-matriculation — NOT a Yale connection as I previously inferred from the family pattern of his Yale-bound great-uncle Laurence Whitney Hooker.

careers.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: senior partner of Hooker & Fay since 1929. Offices at 340 Pine Street, San Francisco; also 1429 Stockton Street SF; 352 Village Lane Los Gatos CA; and 420 South Main Street Salinas CA — a multi-office brokerage operation across central and northern California.

Per Golden Nugget Library: Chairman of finance committee for years 1948 and 1949. The SF Stock Exchange operated as the Pacific Coast's primary regional exchange before its 1957 merger with the Los Angeles Stock Exchange to form the Pacific Coast Stock Exchange.

careers.employer: The Santa Barbara School Alumni Association
careers.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: continued involvement with his prep school after 1921 graduation.

memberships.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library. Continues the family pattern: father Robert Gay Hooker (H04266) and great-uncles Charles Gay (H02703) + C. Osgood (H04265) were all PUC members in the 1905 directory.

sources.short: Tony Hooker pedigree (descendant chart for Thomas Hooker)
1955 Queen Mary passenger list (passport #56725)

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter of Armour & Co Vice-President
narrative_blocks.body: Josephine Dunham was the daughter of Robert James Dunham (1876-1948), Vice-President of Armour and Company, Director of the Continental and Commercial National Bank, and an officer or director of numerous Chicago corporations (per the 1919 Chicago Blue Book). Family residence was 208 South LaSalle Street, Chicago. The Dunhams were among the city's prominent business families.

narrative_blocks.header: First married Rand McNally heir at nineteen
narrative_blocks.body: In October 1925, age 19, Josephine married Harry Beach Clow Jr. (1901-2000), Yale 1924 graduate and grandson of both the Rand McNally publishing dynasty AND the Clow Corporation pipe-and-valve manufacturing dynasty that built much of Chicago's sewer, water, and fire-hydrant infrastructure. Clow worked for Rand McNally from 1924, rising to vice-president and serving on the board into the 1980s; he was on the Clow Corp board 1933-1980. Known socially as 'Firecracker Harry' for his pyrotechnic party displays.

narrative_blocks.header: Married John Rodman Hooker as her second
narrative_blocks.body: By the 1934 SS Santa Rosa voyage SF→NY, Josephine had married John Rodman Hooker, her second husband. Their address by 1934 was 207 El Cerrito Ave, San Mateo CA. By 1955 they had moved to 15650 Arnold Rd, Sonoma.

burial.plot_notes: Cremated. Ashes scattered over the desert outside of Palm Springs CA, privately (per Tony Hooker pedigree).

death.note: Per Tony Hooker pedigree + Sam's data: lived Hillsborough San Mateo County ~10 years before death; lived San Mateo CA in 1939

burial.plot_notes: Cremated at Cypress Lawn Memorial Park Colma but NOT INTERRED there — disposition of cremains uncertain. Per Findagrave: 'Per Cypress Lawn Records, Robert Hooker was cremated at their facility but not interred there. You can call the office at 650-755-0580 to inquire of the disposition of cremains. Perhaps these children of Charles are in the family Mausoleum???'

careers.role: Real estate agent / house broker / rent collector / insurance agent
careers.employer: Hooker & Lent (Robert G. Hooker + George H. Lent)
careers.location: 14 Post Street, San Francisco CA (telephone: Private Exchange 120)
careers.notes: Real estate agency, house brokerage, rent collection, and insurance.

Real estate and investment firm. Founded with brother-in-law George Heydenfeldt Lent (X02298, husband of his sister Bessie H04268) and brother-in-law Eugene Lent. The firm operated through at least 1932 per SF Real Estate Board Yearbook; building referenced in 1916 SF Commercial Club records. Robert G. Hooker was a Pacific-Union Club member (1932 roster) — same as his Lent business partners.
Per 1880s SF directory: 'Hooker Robert G., cashier Am. Sugar Refinery Co., r. 917 Bush' — early-career position before founding Hooker & Lent.

memberships.notes: One of the most exclusive private clubs in the western US. Robert was a member alongside his Lent business partners (his sister Bessie's husband George Heydenfeldt Lent and brother-in-law Eugene Lent).
One of the most exclusive private clubs in the western US. Per SFgenealogy 1932 roster.
Per Bohemian Club Officers & Members 1892 roster: 'HOOKER, Robert G.' — alongside brother G. Osgood Hooker and brother-in-law George H. Lent (married Bessie that same year).

Per Burlingame Country Club roster: 'HOOKER. ROBERT G.. Cunard Bldg.. 503 Market St.. San Francisco. Calif.' — listed alongside several other Hooker family members including 'Lent Hooker' (likely Rodman Lent Hooker HD3246), 'John R. Hooker' (likely John Rodman Hooker HD3243 at Curran & Dwyer), 'Osgood Hooker' (likely Osgood Hooker H05205 — C. Osgood's son), 'Mrs. C. Osgood Hooker' (Ella Wall Goodfellow Hooker, his sister-in-law widow), and 'Robert G. Hooker Jr.' (660 Market Street, Gladding McBean & Co).

narrative_blocks.header: Real estate broker with two brothers-in-law
narrative_blocks.body: He partnered with George and Eugene Lent in the Hooker & Lent firm at 503 Market Street in San Francisco. The firm operated through 1932 and built a long-running family-business network.

narrative_blocks.header: Studied mechanics at Berkeley, ended in real estate
narrative_blocks.body: He took a Ph.B. in Mechanics at the University of California, Berkeley. After early years as cashier at the American Sugar Refining Company, he settled into real estate brokerage.

narrative_blocks.header: Member of San Francisco's three elite clubs
narrative_blocks.body: He belonged to the Bohemian Club from 1892, the Pacific-Union Club, and the Burlingame Country Club. The Hooker family appeared on the Burlingame roster across three generations.

cross_connections.display_label: Brothers-in-law and partners in Hooker & Lent real estate.
Three brothers-in-law ran Hooker & Lent real estate firm.

careers.notes: Per Chicago Blue Book 1919: 'Vice-President of Armour and Company; Director of the Continental and Commercial National Bank; and an officer and director in numerous corporations.' Office address 208 South LaSalle Street, Chicago. Armour and Company was one of the major Chicago meat-packing firms — among the largest American food companies of its era.

INST002.name: Harvard College
INST002.notes: Rev. Thomas Shepard (I00002) was instrumental in founding Harvard and placing it at Cambridge. Rev. Samuel Shepard (H00008) graduated 1658; Samuel Shepard grandson (H00038) graduated 1685; Col. Roger Newton (H00044) also a graduate.
